vulnerability.reference: Relevance bias makes rankers over-prioritize topical similarity, missing disguised harmful intent.
vulnerability.generated: Ranking-based moderation relies on surface-level semantic patterns, rendering it blind to harmful prompts that mimic benign queries.

impact.reference: Jailbreak prompts evade filters, enabling large-scale harmful output generation by adversaries.
impact.generated: Jailbreak prompts bypass safety filters, causing unconstrained generation of high-risk content at scale.

dose_response.reference: Ranking-based harmfulness evaluation relying on cognitive biases causes relevance bias to under-rank harmful prompts in content moderation systems, enabling large-scale harmful output generation by adversaries.
dose_response.generated: Ranking-based harmfulness evaluation biased by simplicity and relevance effects, through content moderation systems, triggers surface-level pattern blindness to harmful prompts mimicking benign queries, leading to jailbreak prompts bypassing filters and generating high-risk content at scale.
